title: Xenial Peppery Norwegian Tempeh En Croute
description: A peppery and romantic Norwegian dish featuring tempeh en croute, served on a wooden board, perfect for a Buddhist temple gathering.

ingredients.name: tempeh
ingredients.notes: marinated in a mixture of soy sauce and maple syrup

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a large rectangle.
Place the marinated tempeh in the center of the pastry rectangle, leaving a 1 inch border around it.
Sprinkle the norwegian cheese, black pepper, thyme, salt, and garlic powder over the tempeh.
Brush the edges of the pastry with olive oil.
Fold the pastry over the tempeh, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the tempeh en croute on a wooden board and brush the top with olive oil.
Bake the tempeh en croute in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the tempeh en croute from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
